resources.string: LetвЂ™s create your account first so we can save progress and recommendations.
These numbers help us estimate your calorie needs and macro targets more accurately.
Choose your activity level and food style. This directly affects recommendations and your daily targets.
These choices shape which cuisines show up more often and which ones the planner should de-prioritize.
Optional. These are treated as strict negative filters so risky dishes stop appearing in recommendations.
Set your target weight so we can build a more realistic daily plan.
Here is your starting daily plan. After sign-up you will land directly in your personalized app.
After registration we will open the app with your profile ready.
Account created, but automatic login failed
Password must contain at least 8 characters
Enter a height of at least 100 cm
Enter a weight of at least 30 kg
Enter a target weight of at least 30 kg
Password must contain at least 8 characters
Retry to refresh calories, meals, recommendations, and daily targets.
Recommendations will appear after a bit more diary activity.
Connect Health Connect so activity, steps, and water targets feel more personal.
Install or update Health Connect to sync steps, activity, weight, and sleep.
Mangajo uses steps, activity, weight, and sleep to adjust targets and coach recommendations.
Daily targets can now adapt to your activity.
Your fasting timer is done. Log your next meal when you are ready.
Calories, hydration, activity, and recommendations in one place.
Log breakfast, lunch, dinner, or snacks to unlock more precise advice.
Nothing logged for this day yet
Add your first meal to start tracking calories and macros.
Search products, scan a barcode, or recognize food from camera.
Use barcode scanning for packaged foods or camera recognition to start a product search from what you see.
Try again, scan a barcode, or switch to manual search.
Try another query, scan the package barcode, or use camera recognition.
This barcode is not in the catalog yet. Try a text search or recognize the product by camera.
Balanced product. Fits well into your daily plan.
High in protein. Great fit when you need a more filling option.
Calorie-dense and carb-heavy. Better suited for earlier meals or active days.
High in fat. Keep an eye on the rest of the dayвЂ™s choices.
No alternatives yet. We will show smarter swaps as the catalog grows.
Point the camera at the barcode
We need camera permission to scan barcodes and return the result directly into product search.
Point the camera at the foodвЂ¦
Choose the most relevant label to continue the product search.
We need camera permission to recognize food and suggest search terms.
Browse recipes, filter by meal type, or jump straight to a meal plan.
These recipes are ranked from the products already in your pantry.
Try another ingredient, clear filters, or generate a meal plan based on your goals.
Check connection and try again. If this keeps happening, recommendations from your history may still appear later.
Compare the regular ranking, exploration mode, and the ML baseline reranker.
Need ideas for the whole day?
Generate a ready-to-use meal plan and open the shopping list right after.
No strong pantry matches yet. Add more products or open the full recipe catalog.
These recipes are ranked from your history, nutrition gaps, and current context.
Showing regular dishes in this category. Personalized reasons only appear on recommended cards.
These are regular category results, separate from personalized recommendations.
Side dish is not fully included
Below you will find the calorie, protein, fat, and carb breakdown for the full recipe and per serving.
Servings calculated: %1$d portions, based on %2$d g per portion
%1$s. Side dish %2$s is not yet included in nutrition values.
Full breakdown of calories, protein, fat, carbs, and other nutritional values.
kcal = protein x 4 + carbs x 4 + fat x 9
Nutrition below is for %1$s serving out of %2$d total servings.
Ingredient data is not available yet.
Protein %1$s g \u2022 fat %2$s g \u2022 carbs %3$s g
Enter how many grams you want to log. Nutrition will be recalculated automatically.
Enter grams, nutrition will be adjusted.
Could not add recipe to diary
Could not add recipe to meal plan
Instructions are not available for this recipe.
Recommended by the personalized AI engine.
Recommended based on your pantry and fridge.
Recommended for your current meal plan.
Generate a plan and we will fill each meal slot with personalized recommendations.
Something went wrong loading the plan. Try refreshing or generate a new one.
No meals planned for this day yet.
Could not log all meals for this day
Recipe will be shown less often
Meal was logged, but replanning failed for some days
Upcoming days updated with pinned meals
Review the plan, swap meals you don\'t like, and confirm when ready.
No recipe selected for this slot yet.
Empty slots for this day filled
Could not load nutrition data for this meal
Per recipe serving: %1$s kcal \u2022 P %2$sg \u2022 F %3$sg \u2022 C %4$sg
Per 100 g: %1$s kcal \u2022 P %2$sg \u2022 F %3$sg \u2022 C %4$sg
What the planner learned from your habits
%1$s usually works better for you when it is faster, around %2$d min. Confidence: %3$s.
%1$s tends to fit you better when protein is around %2$d g. Confidence: %3$s.
%1$s is drifting toward meals around %2$d kcal. Confidence: %3$s.
%1$s is often adjusted in the direction of "%2$s". Confidence: %3$s.
The planner is already adapting %1$s to your recent actions.
Could not load scoring explanation for this meal
Generate a meal plan and we will build the ingredient list automatically.
Refresh the latest plan or generate a new one first.
Meal plan not found. Generate one from Recipes first.
We will assemble a practical plan from your goals and diet, then prepare the shopping list.
This changes the shopping list scale without changing your personal calorie target.
Choose how many servings of the same dish to cook at once. The extra servings will be repeated later in the plan.
For example, you can repeat only lunches and dinners.
Optional. If selected, the plan will lean toward these cuisines without locking itself to them.
The generator will lean on these selected products when it builds your meals.
Retry after refreshing local and server data.
Keep logging meals, water, and weight to see useful trends here.
The main signals about your progress, habits, and how well the planner fits your routine.
Average daily intake over the last 7 days.
Average hydration across recent active days.
How confidently the planner already reads your habits.
Main patterns and quick takeaways from the last 7 days.
No extra recommendations yet. Keep logging meals so insights become more specific.
Start logging meals to see analytics insights.
Your average calorie intake is above the target range.
Your nutrition pattern looks stable. Keep it up.
There is not enough recommendation traffic yet for a stable quality conclusion.
Recommendation quality looks strong: users not only open suggestions, but also add them to the diary at a healthy rate.
Users are opening recommendations well, but conversion to diary logs can still improve.
Recommendation engagement is still modest. This is a good place to compare rule-based ranking with the ML baseline next.
These are the strongest user signals for meal-plan personalization over the last 30 days.
What the app learned about you
These short conclusions are based on meal-plan actions such as keeping, replacing, and logging meals.
Not enough actions yet. Generate a few plans and interact with them so the app can learn your preferences.
%1$s usually trends toward faster meals, around %2$d min. Confidence: %3$s.
%1$s tends to work better for you when protein is around %2$d g. Confidence: %3$s.
%1$s is drifting toward meals around %2$d kcal. Confidence: %3$s.
Generate and use a few plans before judging meal-plan quality.
Users are still adjusting meal plans often. This is useful learning data, but it also means the planner still has room to fit habits better.
Some recipes are repeating often. The planner should keep increasing variety as more feedback arrives.
The planner is already learning from your replacements and can adapt future slots.
Meal plans look stable so far. Keep logging planned meals to make personalization sharper.
How often planned meals are actually logged
How often suggested meals get replaced
These are your explicit profile choices. They influence generation even before enough behavioral data is collected.
This is how your actions shape future meal plans.
Not enough clear signals yet. Generate plans, replace meals, pin good ones, and log what you actually ate.
These are the strongest learned signals for each type of meal.
The planner will prioritize cuisines like %1$s. Total selected cuisines: %2$d.
Cuisines like %1$s are penalized during selection. Total avoided cuisines: %2$d.
Ingredients like %1$s are treated as strict filters. Total allergy signals: %2$d.
%1$d favorite products can appear more often in similar dishes and combinations.
%1$d disliked products and %2$d allergy products are used as negative filters during selection.
You most often replace meals because of "%1$s". This signal appeared %2$d times.
%1$s is drifting toward faster recipes, around %2$d min. Confidence: %3$s.
%1$s tends to work better when protein is around %2$d g. Confidence: %3$s.
%1$s is trending toward meals around %2$d kcal. Confidence: %3$s.
%1$s is mostly affected by the reason "%2$s". Confidence: %3$s.
Pinned meals: %1$d. Manually added meals: %2$d. These actions are treated as strong positive feedback.
Repeats are also part of the signal
The recipe "%1$s" repeated %2$d times, so the planner is tracking whether repetition is acceptable for you.
Start logging meals so the coach can analyze your nutrition pattern.
Add more protein-rich foods such as cottage cheese, eggs, or meat.
Too many processed foods. Try cooking at home more often.
Excellent nutrition pattern. You are giving your body what it needs.
Good momentum, with a few areas that can still improve.
Your nutrition pattern needs adjustment to better support your goals.
Target weight is currently read-only and comes from the server profile.
Enter a valid weight greater than 0
Enter a valid target weight greater than 0
Changing target weight updates your profile goal calculations.
Manage disliked foods and allergy products for safer recommendations.
Preferences are synced with your profile and also kept locally for a faster interface.
Choose foods you do not want to see in plans.
Choose foods that should be treated as allergens.
Store products you already have at home.
No products in the fridge yet.
Quick access to saved products and recipes.
You do not have favorite products yet.
You do not have favorite recipes yet.
Recipe weight is taken from the selected serving.
Save frequent meals and add them to your diary in one tap.
Use the email you registered with.
We\'ve sent a verification link to your email. Click the link in the email, then come back and sign in.
Please verify your email before logging in
Create your account. You can fill in the remaining profile details in the next steps.
Already have an account? Sign in
An account with this email already exists
Too many attempts. Please try again a little later
Password must contain at least 8 characters
Use an email address you can access.
You have not reached your water goal for today yet. A glass of water right now can help you feel better.
Enter dish details if it is not in our catalog yet. We will save it directly into your diary.
You have had less than a liter of water today. A glass of water right now can help keep your day on track.
You slept less than six hours. Keep meals steady today and favor simple protein-rich choices.
For several days in a row, protein has stayed below target. Add a reliable protein source earlier in the day.
You have been finishing your fasting windows consistently. That steady routine is a real asset for the rest of the plan.
Fasting window may be too aggressive
Recent fasting sessions are ending well below the selected target. A slightly shorter window could be easier to sustain.
Recent dinners are taking a big share of your daily calories. A lighter dinner may make the day feel easier.
You already burned about %1$d active kcal today. A balanced meal now should fit your day well.
You are close to completing today’s fasting target. Plan a calm first meal with protein and water.
Today most calories are coming without enough protein. Add cottage cheese, eggs, or chicken to your next meal.
You have about %1$d kcal left. Good window for a light dinner without going over.
You have already reached your calorie goal. If you are still hungry, water or something very light is the better choice.
Your nutrition pattern today looks fairly balanced. Keep it up.
Choose products you want the planner to use more often.
Pick cuisines you want to see more often in meal plans.
Pick cuisines you want to see less often in meal plans.
These cuisines softly influence meal-plan generation. You can leave this empty and generate without a cuisine bias.
These cuisines are not fully banned, but the planner will try to show them less often.
Favorite products are treated as positive taste signals and can appear more often in recommendations and meal plans.
Disliked products are filtered out from plans whenever possible, so the generator stops offering things you do not want to eat.
Allergy products act as the strictest filter and should be blocked from meal recommendations for safer planning.
Food preferences: %1$d favorites, %2$d disliked, %3$d allergies, %4$d cuisines
Optional. These choices help the planner lean toward recipes you are more likely to enjoy.
Optional. These cuisines are not banned, but the planner will try to show them less often.
